name: user-prompt-submit
context_prose: user-prompt-submit.sh is the simplest of the three engram hooks. Claude Code execs it on every UserPromptSubmit lifecycle event (registered via R3 in hooks.json with a 5s timeout). The script emits a single fixed JSON document on stdout via `jq -n`, populating `hookSpecificOutput.hookEventName` with the literal `"UserPromptSubmit"` and `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` with a constant reminder string nudging the agent to call `/learn` at completion boundaries and `/prepare` at new-work boundaries. The script reads no stdin, performs no I/O beyond stdout, exits non-zero on any failure courtesy of `set -euo pipefail`, and has no inputs that influence its output — every invocation emits the same bytes.

properties.name: strict-bash-mode
properties.statement: Script runs under `set -euo pipefail`, so any command failure, unset variable, or pipe failure aborts with non-zero exit before stdout is emitted.

properties.name: fixed-event-name
properties.statement: Emitted JSON has `hookSpecificOutput.hookEventName` set to the literal string `"UserPromptSubmit"`, matching the lifecycle event Claude Code dispatched.

properties.name: constant-reminder-payload
properties.statement: `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` is a constant string nudging the agent to call `/learn` at completion boundaries (task done, bug resolved, direction change, commit) and `/prepare` when starting new work; nothing in the input or environment alters its content.

properties.name: single-json-document-on-stdout
properties.statement: Script writes exactly one well-formed JSON document to stdout (via `jq -n`) and writes nothing else, so Claude Code's stdout-JSON consumer (R7) always receives a parseable payload.

properties.name: no-stdin-no-side-effects
properties.statement: Script reads no stdin, performs no file I/O, spawns no background work, and depends only on `jq` being on PATH; output is a pure function of the script source.

properties.name: fits-within-5s-timeout
properties.statement: Single `jq -n` invocation completes well within the 5s timeout registered by R3 in hooks.json, so Claude Code never has to kill the hook for taking too long.
